---
title: "Functional connectivity"
subtitle: "Seed-to-target correlations and Fisher z transforms."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Seed-Based Correlations

Functional connectivity analyses often start from a seed time series and a
matrix of target time series. This page keeps the calculation explicit and
uses `fmrimod` for common effect-size transforms. It is an auxiliary summary
example, not a replacement for the first-level and group-modeling seam used by
the flagship workflows.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.residualize import residualize

rng = np.random.default_rng(13)
n_time = 160
n_targets = 8

seed = rng.normal(size=n_time)
targets = rng.normal(size=(n_time, n_targets))
targets[:, :3] += 0.45 * seed[:, None]

corr = np.array([np.corrcoef(seed, targets[:, j])[0, 1] for j in range(n_targets)])
z, z_var = fm.r_to_z(corr, n_time)

pd.DataFrame({
    "target": [f"roi_{j + 1}" for j in range(n_targets)],
    "r": corr,
    "fisher_z": z,
    "z_variance": z_var,
}).round(3)
```

## Return to Correlation Units

Fisher z values are useful for group summaries because they are closer to
normal. Convert back to correlations for presentation.

```{python}
pooled_z = z.mean()
pooled_r = fm.z_to_r(pooled_z)

round(float(pooled_z), 3), round(float(pooled_r), 3)
```

## Residualize an Analysis Signal

For already preprocessed time series, residualize the seed and target signals
with respect to explicit nuisance columns before correlation. Motion
correction, registration, and normalization remain outside fmrimod's scope.

```{python}
nuisance = np.column_stack([np.ones(n_time), np.linspace(-1, 1, n_time)])
seed_resid = residualize(nuisance, seed[:, None]).ravel()
target_resid = residualize(nuisance, targets)

partial_corr = np.array([
    np.corrcoef(seed_resid, target_resid[:, j])[0, 1]
    for j in range(n_targets)
])

pd.DataFrame({"raw_r": corr, "residualized_r": partial_corr}).round(3)
```

## Next

- [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
- [Datasets](datasets.qmd)
